title: Baby Back Ribs
description: Low-and-slow oven-baked baby back ribs with a brown sugar dry rub, finished with homemade BBQ sauce. Tender, fall-off-the-bone, deeply smoky.

ingredients.name: apple juice or cider vinegar (for foil wrap)
cayenne or hot sauce, optional (BBQ sauce)

instructions.text: Make the dry rub: mix brown sugar, paprika, salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and cayenne in a small bowl. Remove the membrane from the back of the ribs. Pat ribs dry and coat both sides with the rub. Wrap tightly in foil with a splash of apple juice or cider vinegar. Refrigerate until cook time or leave at room temp if cooking within an hour.
Preheat oven to 275°F. Place wrapped ribs on a baking sheet and bake for 2.5 hours.
Make the BBQ sauce: combine all sauce ingredients in a saucepan over low heat. Simmer 10–15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Taste and adjust seasoning. Let cool slightly.
Remove ribs from oven. Carefully open foil -- hot steam will escape. Brush generously with BBQ sauce. Return to oven unwrapped at 375°F for 15–20 minutes until the glaze caramelizes.
Rest 5–10 minutes before cutting into individual ribs. Serve with extra BBQ sauce alongside.

notes:
Removing the membrane from the back of the rack is important -- it prevents the rub from penetrating and makes the finished ribs tough to eat.

The foil wrap with liquid is what keeps the ribs moist during the long low-heat bake. Don't skip it.

For a smokier flavor without a smoker, add 1/2 tsp liquid smoke to the foil packet.
